steps.title: Calculate the total volume of the cylindrical can
steps.content:
The volume of a cylinder is found by multiplying the base area by the height. With a base area of 75 cm² and height of 10 cm:

$$V_{\text{can}} = 75 \times 10 = 750 \text{ cm}^3$$

steps.title: Subtract the syrup volume to find the fruit volume
steps.content: The can is filled to the top with both syrup and fruit. If 110 cm³ of syrup occupies part of the 750 cm³ total, then the pieces of fruit occupy the remaining volume: $$V_{\text{fruit}} = V_{\text{can}} - V_{\text{syrup}} = 750 - 110 = 640 \text{ cm}^3$$ That matches choice C — C is correct.
